name: block-push-to-main.js
purpose: Blocks git push commands targeting main or master branches by inspecting the Bash tool_input.command string; exits 2 to abort, 0 to allow.
notes: Does NOT use scripts/lib helpers — appropriate since it never writes files or logs errors to disk; fail-open on parse errors. CRITICAL_HOOKS list in settings-guardian.js includes this file.

name: check-mcp-servers.js
purpose: Reads .mcp.json at session start and prints available MCP server names to stdout (no URLs, tokens, or secrets exposed); dynamic — no hardcoded server names.
notes: Does NOT use scripts/lib helpers — implements its own inline path-traversal guard and output sanitizer. Scope is 'project' because it reads a project-local .mcp.json; the logic itself is fully portable. The server-name list varies per project.

name: commit-tracker.js
purpose: PostToolUse hook (Bash) that detects new git commits, appends structured metadata to .claude/state/commit-log.jsonl (Layer A compaction resilience), logs commit failures to commit-failures.jsonl, surfaces pre-commit hook output, and spawns mid-session health alerts.
notes: Largest hook at 21KB; uses ALL three scripts/lib helpers (safe-fs, security-helpers, sanitize-error via security-helpers). References SESSION_CONTEXT.md for session counter — the counter format is a convention that downstream projects must adopt or the counter returns null (gracefully handled). mid-session-alerts.js is an OPTIONAL dependency — missing file is silently skipped.

name: compact-restore.js
purpose: SessionStart hook (compact matcher) that reads .claude/state/handoff.json written by pre-compaction-save.js and injects structured recovery context into Claude's context window after compaction.
notes: Only fires when session resumes under the 'compact' matcher — not on ordinary SessionStart. Stale handoff (>60 min) is silently skipped. Does NOT use safe-fs or sanitize-error from scripts/lib directly — uses security-helpers.js and sanitize-input from lib/. fs.readFileSync is called without safe-fs wrapper (the read-path fallback), which is noted as a minor gap vs CLAUDE.md §2 policy.

name: large-file-gate.js
purpose: PreToolUse gate on Read operations that blocks reads of files >5 MB and warns on files >500 KB, logging to hook-warnings-log.jsonl; respects SKIP_GATES=1 kill switch.
notes: Uses sanitize-error.cjs (hard require, not try/catch) and safe-fs (soft require with fallback). Correct path-traversal guard pattern (/^\.\.\./).test()) per CLAUDE.md §5. SKIP_GATES=1 env var bypasses all checks — useful for debugging.

name: pre-compaction-save.js
purpose: PreCompact hook that captures a comprehensive state snapshot (task states, commit log, git context, agent summaries, active plan, session notes, active audits) to .claude/state/handoff.json before context compaction.
notes: Second largest hook at 16.7KB. Uses state-utils.saveJson (atomic write with symlink guard) for the handoff output. Reads settings.json to check CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS flag — this creates a soft coupling to the settings.json schema. References SESSION_CONTEXT.md session counter convention (same as commit-tracker). Does NOT use sanitize-error from scripts/lib — only uses JASON-OS lib helpers.

name: run-node.sh
purpose: Bash wrapper that resolves the Node.js binary across multiple install locations (PATH, fnm, nvm-windows, Homebrew, system) and execs the specified hook script; prevents path-traversal via case-match guards on the script argument.

existing_metadata.event: (shared launcher — invoked by all other hooks via settings.json commands)

notes: Not itself wired as a hook event; it is the command prefix used in every hook's 'command' field (e.g. 'bash .claude/hooks/run-node.sh block-push-to-main.js'). Contains Windows-specific logic (fnm_multishells, LOCALAPPDATA, .cmd/.bat dispatch via cmd.exe) but gracefully handles macOS/Linux too — fully cross-platform. Path-traversal guard rejects '..' segments and backslashes in script arg. Refuses to execute symlinked scripts.

name: settings-guardian.js
purpose: PreToolUse hook (Write/Edit) that blocks modifications to .claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.local.json that would remove critical hooks (block-push-to-main.js, settings-guardian.js itself); appends blocks to hook-warnings-log.jsonl.
notes: CRITICAL_HOOKS list is hardcoded to ['block-push-to-main.js','settings-guardian.js'] — when porting, this list must be updated if hook names differ in the target project. continueOnError is absent (defaults to false/blocking). Uses sanitize-error.cjs (hard require) and safe-fs (soft require with fallback). The 'sanitize-then-portable' portability rating reflects the CRITICAL_HOOKS hardcoded names.

name: git-utils.js
purpose: Shared utility resolving the project directory from CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env (with bidirectional containment check and 10-level ancestor depth limit) and providing gitExec() for safe execFileSync git calls.
notes: Used by commit-tracker.js and pre-compaction-save.js. No scripts/lib dependency — self-contained. The resolveProjectDir() function is the canonical project-dir resolver for all hooks that use this lib.

name: rotate-state.js
purpose: Shared JSONL/JSON state file rotation helpers: rotateJsonl (cap JSONL to N entries), pruneJsonKey (cap array within JSON), expireByAge / expireJsonlByAge (time-based expiry), archiveRotateJsonl (rotate with archive file + ack-aware eviction).
notes: Largest lib file at 16.7KB; proto-pollution guard (PROTO_POLLUTION_KEYS set) on pruneJsonKey. Uses backup-swap atomic write pattern throughout. Includes advisory file locking via safe-fs withLock() in archiveRotateJsonl. Fully uses scripts/lib helpers.

name: sanitize-input.js
purpose: Shared input sanitization: strips control characters, redacts secret patterns (tokens, Bearer, Basic auth, key=value with sensitive names), and truncates to configurable max length.
notes: Used by commit-tracker.js and compact-restore.js. Does NOT depend on scripts/lib — self-contained. SECRET_PATTERNS array is the single source of truth for secret redaction in hook layer.

name: state-utils.js
purpose: Shared JSON state persistence: loadJson (parse JSON file, null on error) and saveJson (atomic tmp+backup write with symlink guard, restricted to .claude/state/ and .claude/hooks/ directories).
notes: saveJson enforces a hard allowed-roots check (.claude/state/ and .claude/hooks/) — writes outside these dirs are silently rejected. This is a security-by-design constraint; importing projects must keep state within those paths or extend the allowedRoots list.

name: symlink-guard.js
purpose: isSafeToWrite() utility that checks a file path and all ancestor directories for symlinks before allowing writes; fail-closed (returns false on any error or symlink detected).
notes: Smallest lib file (1.4KB). Used transitively by nearly all hooks via state-utils.js and rotate-state.js. Pure path-walking logic — no project-specific references.

name: settings.json hooks summary
purpose: Central hook event wiring; maps Claude Code lifecycle events to run-node.sh invocations.
notes: All hook commands are prefixed with 'bash .claude/hooks/run-node.sh' — run-node.sh is the universal launcher. Two hooks have continueOnError:false (block-push-to-main, settings-guardian) — these are hard-blocking gates. Three hooks have continueOnError:true — fail-open by design. commit-tracker has no 'if:' condition so it fires on every Bash call (fast-bailed internally by regex). Only one PreToolUse hook has an 'if:' condition (block-push-to-main). No Stop or SubagentStop events are wired.
